texto: it is all clearer now than it was then. Rhea would say it is the vivid fabrications of an ageing mind. More likely, though, it is the clarity that comes from ageing - from the natural process of releasing the mind from imagined futures, and allow the present and paste to take their rightful place at the centre of our attention. The past is palpable to Sheldon now, in the way the future is to the young. It is either a brief curse or a gift before oblivion.
explicacao: Citação de Derek B. Miller sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “it is all clearer now than it was then. Rhea would say it is the vivid fabrications of an ageing …” articula reflexao, ageing, future em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como future e past.

texto: Everyone gets killed in the shower. Don't you go to the movies? Psycho. Dead in shower. The MExican in No country for Old Men. Dead in shower. Michelle Pfeiffer in What Lies Beneath. Almost dead in shower, or in the bath, anyway. But she did that thing with her toe and got out OD. Still the shower, though...Glen Close in Fatal Attraction. Dead in shower. John Travolta in Pulp Fiction. Very dead in shower. But never closets. I can't think of anyone shot in a closet. This is why I hide in closets.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Derek B. Miller que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “Everyone gets killed in the shower. Don't you go to the movies? Psycho. Dead in shower. The MExic…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, closet, death, com foco em luto e death.
